title: quamoclit coccinea var. luteola
intro: Quamoclit coccinea var. luteola, also known as the yellow wood rose or yellow clematis, is a flowering vine native to tropical Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Quamoclit coccinea var. luteola are attributed to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
